# dynasimR — Dynamic Agent-Node Simulation Analysis

A domain-neutral analysis and visualisation layer for discrete-event,
agent-based and node-actor simulation outputs. The package is
schema-harmonised so that two output profiles (**Profile A** and
**Profile B**) can be analysed with a single API.

Typical use cases include any system in which entities (actors, nodes,
agents) move through states and interact within spatial or network
environments — for example flow analyses, state-transition studies,
resource allocation and policy comparisons.

## Installation

``` r
# install.packages("remotes")
remotes::install_github("rabanheller/dynasimR")
```

## Quick start

``` r
library(dynasimR)

# 1. Load bundled example data (or point to your own simulation outputs)
sim <- load_example_data()
# sim <- read_simulation("~/my-simulation/data/raw/")

# 2. Time-to-event analysis
km <- km_estimate(sim, endpoint = "stage2", stratify_by = "scenario")
plot_km(km, title = "Time-to-stage-2")

# 3. Policy effect (policy A vs. policy B)
pol <- policy_effect(sim, policy_a_scenario = "A-S08",
                     policy_b_scenario = "A-S07")
cat(pol$narrative)   # ready to drop into a report

# 4. Autonomy-level trade-off
al <- al_efficiency(sim)
plot_al_tradeoff(al)

# 5. Manuscript export
export_latex_table(
  data     = pol$effect_sizes,
  filename = "policy_table.tex",
  caption  = "Policy effect sizes.",
  label    = "policy"
)

# 6. Interactive Shiny dashboard
launch_app()
```

## Feature map

| Analysis goal                         | Key function                                                                                                                                                       |
|---------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Entity flow through processing stages | `stage_throughput()`                                                                                                                                               |
| Time-to-event stratified by scenario  | [`km_estimate()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/km_estimate.md), [`cox_model()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/cox_model.md) |
| Policy A vs. policy B comparison      | `policy_effect()`                                                                                                                                                  |
| Autonomy-level trade-off (AL0-AL5)    | [`al_efficiency()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/al_efficiency.md)                                                                             |
| Compliance Index                      | `compute_compliance_index()`                                                                                                                                       |
| Profile B progress trajectory         | `progress_trajectory()`                                                                                                                                            |
| Profile B wait-gap index              | `compute_wait_gap_index()`                                                                                                                                         |
| Manuscript placeholder fill           | [`fill_placeholders()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/fill_placeholders.md)                                                                     |

## Requirements

- R \>= 4.1.0 (base pipe `|>` used throughout)
- Required imports: dplyr, tidyr, purrr, readr, tibble, ggplot2,
  survival, rlang, cli, glue
- Shiny app requires the `Suggests:` dependencies — install with
  [`dynasimR::check_app_dependencies()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/check_app_dependencies.md)

## License

MIT (c) R. Heller

# Package index

## Data I/O

Load, validate and inspect simulation outputs.

- [`read_simulation()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/read_simulation.md)
  : Load simulation outputs into the dynasimR standard format
- [`load_example_data()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/load_example_data.md)
  : Load the package-bundled example dataset
- [`validate_dynasimR_data()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/validate_dynasimR_data.md)
  : Validate a dynasimR_data object
- [`scenario_meta`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/scenario_meta.md)
  : Scenario metadata for MEDTACS-SIM and REHASIM
- [`survival_params`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/survival_params.md)
  : Weibull survival parameters by triage category

## Survival analysis

Kaplan-Meier, Cox regression and associated plots.

- [`km_estimate()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/km_estimate.md)
  : Kaplan-Meier estimator for simulation data
- [`cox_model()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/cox_model.md)
  : Cox proportional-hazards model for simulation data
- [`plot_km()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/plot_km.md)
  : Kaplan-Meier survival curves
- [`plot_forest()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/plot_forest.md)
  : Forest plot of Cox hazard ratios

## Doctrine effects

MUF vs. Military Necessity quantification.

- [`doctrine_effect()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/doctrine_effect.md)
  : Quantify doctrine effect (MUF vs. Military Necessity)
- [`plot_doctrine()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/plot_doctrine.md)
  : Doctrine effect bar chart with CI whiskers

## Autonomy trade-off

AL0-AL5 survival-vs-IHL analysis.

- [`al_efficiency()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/al_efficiency.md)
  : Compute AL-Efficiency ratio (survival advantage vs. IHL compliance)
- [`plot_al_tradeoff()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/plot_al_tradeoff.md)
  : Autonomy-level trade-off plot: KIA reduction vs. IHL compliance
- [`plot_radar()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/plot_radar.md)
  : Radar chart of metrics across scenarios

## IHL / Patient flow

Compliance index, throughput, bottlenecks.

- [`compute_ihl_index()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/compute_ihl_index.md)
  : IHL-Compliance Index
- [`role_throughput()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/role_throughput.md)
  : Role-1 to Role-4 throughput analysis
- [`detect_bottlenecks()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/detect_bottlenecks.md)
  : Bottleneck detection across roles of care
- [`uav_comparison()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/uav_comparison.md)
  : Compare outcomes across UAV configurations

## Sensitivity analysis

- [`morris_screening()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/morris_screening.md)
  : Morris-style sensitivity screening from simulation replicates
- [`tornado_data()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/tornado_data.md)
  : Tornado-diagram data from one-at-a-time perturbation

## REHASIM extensions

Rehabilitation flow analysis.

- [`fim_trajectory_analysis()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/fim_trajectory_analysis.md)
  : FIM trajectory analysis (REHASIM)
- [`compute_waiting_gap_index()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/compute_waiting_gap_index.md)
  : Waiting-Gap Index (REHASIM)
- [`mil_civil_effect()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/mil_civil_effect.md)
  : Military vs. civilian prioritisation effect (REHASIM)
- [`compute_rtd_analysis()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/compute_rtd_analysis.md)
  : Return-to-Duty outcome analysis (multinomial)
- [`spatial_supply_demand()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/spatial_supply_demand.md)
  : Supply-Demand Index (SDI) per region (REHASIM)
- [`plot_fim_curves()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/plot_fim_curves.md)
  : FIM trajectory plot (REHASIM)
- [`plot_sdi_map()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/plot_sdi_map.md)
  : Supply-Demand Index choropleth (REHASIM)
- [`plot_cost_effectiveness()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/plot_cost_effectiveness.md)
  : Cost-effectiveness plane and CEAC curve (REHASIM)

## Visualisation

- [`plot_map()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/plot_map.md)
  : 2D casualty map snapshot
- [`plot_scenario_heatmap()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/plot_scenario_heatmap.md)
  : Scenario comparison heatmap
- [`plot_timeline()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/plot_timeline.md)
  : Time-series plot of a simulation metric
- [`theme_dynasimR()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/theme_dynasimR.md)
  : ggplot2 theme for dynasimR figures
- [`dynasimR_colors()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/dynasimR_colors.md)
  : NATO-inspired colour palette for dynasimR plots
- [`scale_color_identity_dynasimR()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/scale_color_identity_dynasimR.md)
  : Colour scale for combatant identity
- [`scale_fill_identity_dynasimR()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/scale_fill_identity_dynasimR.md)
  : Fill scale for combatant identity
- [`scale_color_doctrine_dynasimR()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/scale_color_doctrine_dynasimR.md)
  : Colour scale for doctrine principle

## Manuscript export

- [`export_figure()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/export_figure.md)
  : Export a ggplot in publication-ready format

- [`export_latex_table()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/export_latex_table.md)
  : Export a tibble as a Springer Nature compatible LaTeX table

- [`fill_placeholders()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/fill_placeholders.md)
  :

  Fill `[XX_*]` placeholders in a LaTeX manuscript

## Shiny dashboard

- [`launch_app()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/launch_app.md)
  : Launch the MEDTACS-SIM Shiny dashboard
- [`check_app_dependencies()`](https://rabanheller.github.io/dynasimR/reference/check_app_dependencies.md)
  : Check availability of Shiny-app dependencies
